required.hint: Number of data-instance copies. Required even though the manifest's validations.required is false — every storage cost expression multiplies by it, so without it the rendered cost stays at $0.
EBS storage type for the cluster (top-level field, NOT the columnFormIPM row's Storage selector — those are different).

traps: Service rebrand: this entry's serviceCode is 'amazonElasticsearchService' but the calculator displays it as 'Amazon OpenSearch Service' — AWS rebranded the service in 2021 but the manifest key kept the legacy name. Both refer to the same product.

Three templates in the manifest: 'elasticSearchService' (provisioned, this entry's templateId), 'elasticSearchService-serverlesss' (serverless OCU pricing — note the 3 s's, not a typo in the manifest), and 'elasticSearchService-serverlesss-Ingestion' (separate ingestion pipeline). minimalConfig targets provisioned; for serverless workloads use a different config shape (numericInputs for indexing OCUs / search OCUs / index size).

Instance configuration uses a columnFormIPM matrix — same WRAPPED shape as RDS: { columnFormIPM_1: { value: [{ <selectorId>: { value: '<n>' }, ... }] } }. Selector ids: 'Number of Nodes Data instance', 'Instance Type', 'Instance Family', 'TermType', 'Storage'. The literal 'undefined' key is the utilization slot: { value: { unit: '<percent>', selectedId: '%Utilized/Month' } }. Sending a bare array `[{...}]` instead of the wrapped form silently saves but the pricing engine ignores the row.

columnFormIPM Instance Family options are NOT what you'd guess. Valid: 'Memory optimized', 'General purpose' (lowercase 'p'), 'Compute optimized', 'Storage optimized', 'OpenSearch Optimized', 'OR1'. Sending 'Standard' or other common AWS labels fails value validation.

columnFormIPM Storage selector is the INSTANCE-ATTACHED storage choice (EBS Only / 1 x 1900 NVMe SSD / etc — 62 options). It is NOT the same as top-level storageType (which is the EBS volume type: GP3 / GP2 / PIOPS Storage / Magnetic). For most workloads use Storage='EBS Only' in the matrix and storageType='GP3' at top level.

There are THREE columnFormIPM tables: columnFormIPM_1 (data instances — primary), columnFormIPM_2 (dedicated master nodes), and columnFormIPM (UltraWarm). When ANY columnFormIPM table is absent from the saved blob, the calculator UI silently rehydrates it with manifest defaults — master defaults to 3× r5.2xlarge.search, UltraWarm to 2× nodes — and prices them in. Production case 2026-06-03 saved with only columnFormIPM_1: rendered $2394/mo for what the user thought was a $280/mo cluster (8.5× phantom cost). Always include all three tables; set Number of Nodes='0' on the ones the user doesn't want. The column-form-default-trap lint predicate catches this at save time.

GP3-specific fields (gp3StorageAmount, gp3ProvisioningIOPS, gp3Throughput) are gated by storageType=GP3. The top-level storageAmount field is for non-GP3 storage types; using both with GP3 selected sends conflicting signals. For GP3 set storageAmount via gp3StorageAmount instead, and add gp3ProvisioningIOPS (default 3000) + gp3Throughput (default 125 MB/s) only if you need above-baseline IOPS or throughput.
